descriptions: Raïa Mutomboki briefly allied with government against M23.
edges.evidence_quote: Colonel Albert Kahasha, who had surrendered and joined government troops along with other leaders of Mai-Mai militia groups Raïa Mutomboki and Nyatura.

descriptions: Raïa Mutomboki was aligned with M23.
edges.evidence_quote: Raïa Mutomboki, aligned with M23

Hell's Lovers Motorcycle Club.description: Multi-ethnic outlaw motorcycle club founded in Chicago in 1967 by Frank 'Claim-Jumper' Rios. Active in various U.S. states with over 1,500 members. Associated with drug trafficking and motorcycle theft, and has been involved in violent incidents with rival clubs.

descriptions: A Marauders member allegedly killed a Hell's Lovers member, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Luther Vaughn Jr., a member of the St. Louis chapter of the Hell's Lovers, was shot and killed at the Jets Clubhouse Lounge, a nightclub in East St. Louis, on March 31, 1985, apparently by a member of the rival Marauders motorcycle club.

descriptions: Threats of violence between Hell's Lovers and Outlaws, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In June 2008, the Ironhorse Roundup Bike Show... was canceled... after state and Federal law enforcement authorities had warned... of threats of violence between the Hell's Lovers and a rival outlaw gang, the Outlaws.

descriptions: A Wheels of Soul member attacked Hell's Lovers clubhouse, and Hell's Lovers returned fire, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On August 2, 2010, a member of the Wheels of Soul fired upon the Hell's Lovers' clubhouse in Denver with a shotgun from a sports utility vehicle. Three Hell's Lovers bikers who were inside the clubhouse returned fire.

descriptions: The text lists Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club as an enemy of Wheels of Soul.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemies of the Wheels of Soul include the Outcast Motorcycle Club, Sin City Titans, Hells Lovers Motorcycle Club and the Street Soldiers.

Los Rojos.description: Los Rojos is a faction of the Gulf Cartel, formed in the late 1990s as an armed wing for security. Based in Reynosa, Tamaulipas, Mexico, it engaged in infighting with the Metros faction after the assassination of Samuel Flores Borrego in 2011. The group is involved in murder and kidnapping, and remains active but less powerful.

descriptions: Beltrán-Leyva Cartel is a rival of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Their primary rivals in Morelos and Guerrero were Jalisco New Generation Cartel , the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , and Guerreros Unidos .

descriptions: Jalisco New Generation Cartel is a rival of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Their primary rivals in Morelos and Guerrero were Jalisco New Generation Cartel , the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , and Guerreros Unidos .

descriptions: Guerreros Unidos is a rival of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Their primary rivals in Morelos and Guerrero were Jalisco New Generation Cartel , the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , and Guerreros Unidos .

descriptions: Los Rojos is a faction of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rojos is a faction of a Mexican drug trafficking organization known as the Gulf Cartel.

descriptions: Los Ardillos is a rival of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Metros , Los Zetas , MS-13 , Los Negros , Jalisco New Generation Cartel , Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , Los Ardillos , Guerreros Unidos

descriptions: Los Rojos ordered the assassination of a Metros leader, triggering conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: On 2 September 2011, Mejía González and Rafael Cárdenas Vela , two leaders of the Rojos, ordered the assassination of the drug lord Samuel Flores Borrego , who commanded the Metros, another faction within the Gulf cartel.

descriptions: Los Mexicles is an ally of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gulf Cartel , Los Mexicles , Sureños , Mexican Mafia , Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Los Negros is a rival of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Metros , Los Zetas , MS-13 , Los Negros , Jalisco New Generation Cartel , Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , Los Ardillos , Guerreros Unidos

descriptions: Los Rojos were suspected of being too soft on Los Zetas, and some members joined Los Zetas.
edges.evidence_quote: Other sources indicate that the infighting could have been caused by the suspicions that the Rojos were 'too soft' on the Gulf cartel's bitter enemy, Los Zetas.

descriptions: MS-13 is a rival of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Metros , Los Zetas , MS-13 , Los Negros , Jalisco New Generation Cartel , Beltrán-Leyva Cartel , Los Ardillos , Guerreros Unidos

descriptions: Mexican Mafia is an ally of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gulf Cartel , Los Mexicles , Sureños , Mexican Mafia , Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel is an ally of Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gulf Cartel , Los Mexicles , Sureños , Mexican Mafia , Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: MOD and MBS are rival gangs that formed from the breakup of Peace MOD.
edges.evidence_quote: the breakup of "Peace MOD" created rivalries between all three of them.

Egan's Rats.description: American organized crime gang based in St. Louis, Missouri, active from 1890 to 1924. Founded by Thomas 'Snake' Kinney and Tom Egan, the gang engaged in bootlegging, labor slugging, voter intimidation, armed robbery, and murder. It was predominantly Irish-American but included Italian and Jewish members. The gang dissolved after key members were imprisoned for a mail robbery in 1924.

descriptions: The Bottoms Gang feuded with Egan's Rats, their primary rival.
edges.evidence_quote: The number-one enemy of the Bottoms Gang was the larger, more powerful Egan's Rats gang.

descriptions: Egan's Rats and Bottoms Gang were rivals, with a war in 1916.
edges.evidence_quote: The Egan Gang's main rival at this time was the violent Bottoms Gang from the Twenty-second Ward.

descriptions: Cuckoo Gang provided information to Egan's Rats for a robbery.
edges.evidence_quote: The Rats took control of the truck and made off with $2.4 million in cash and negotiable bonds after receiving a tip from the Cuckoo Gang.

descriptions: Egan's Rats and Hogan Gang were in a violent conflict after the murder of Willie Egan.
edges.evidence_quote: Egan's Rats soon went to war with the rival Hogan Gang, whom they suspected of killing Willie Egan.

descriptions: Egan's Rats and Hogan Gang fought in the Egan-Hogan War.
edges.evidence_quote: The gangs engaged in the Egan-Hogan War of 1921–23, which resulted in the breakup of Egan's Rats.

descriptions: Green Ones fought with Egan's Rats over bootlegging rackets.
edges.evidence_quote: Giannola attempted to take control of the bootlegging rackets in the area and fought with members of the Egan's Rats gang and Cuckoos Gang.

descriptions: The Hogan Gang and Egan's Rats were engaged in a violent gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: They fought a notoriously violent gang war with Egan's Rats in the early 1920s.

descriptions: The Serb mafia is a rival of Princ Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Princ Gang , UÇK , Naser Gang

descriptions: Johannesburg chapter exported cannabis to British Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: A South African Hells Angel had been arrested in Swaziland for seeking to buy marihuana on a massive scale as the Johannesburg chapter had received an order from the British Hells Angels for marihuana.

descriptions: Imperial Gangsta Bloods is a subdivision of the United Blood Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: the UBN subdivision Imperial Gangsta Bloods (IGB) in Northern Virginia

Dominicans Don't Play.description: Dominicans Don't Play (DDP) is a Dominican-American street gang formed in Manhattan, New York, in 1991. Known for using machetes and knives, they are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang operates across New York City, New Jersey, and internationally, with ties to Colombian cartels for cocaine distribution.

descriptions: DDP and Bloods engaged in a violent altercation.
edges.evidence_quote: A scuffle between DDP and the Bloods at Pelham Prep High School in the Bronx on March 11, 2005, resulted in the stabbing of two teenage boys.

descriptions: DDP and Latin Kings are at peace.
edges.evidence_quote: They also are at peace with the Latin Kings, a Puerto Rican-based gang who has had previous problems with their rivals the Trinitarios.

descriptions: DDP and Trinitarios are rivals since the early 2000s, but were once allied in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The Trinitarios are DDP's biggest rival gang since the early 2000s, they were once united in the 1990s.

descriptions: DDP and Trinitarios were united in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: The Trinitarios are DDP's biggest rival gang since the early 2000s, they were once united in the 1990s.

descriptions: DDP has an ongoing feud with sets affiliated with United Blood Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: They have had an ongoing feud with other area gangs, including the sets affiliated with the United Blood Nation

descriptions: Dominicans Don't Play is listed as a rival of Latin Kings.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips, Dominicans Don't Play, Folk Nation, Gangster Disciples, Latin Eagles, Maniac Latin Disciples, MS-13, Ñetas, La Raza Nation, Simon City Royals, Los Solidos, Spanish Cobras, Tiny Rascal Gang, United Blood Nation

descriptions: Enemy listed in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Dominicans Dont Play

descriptions: Infobox lists Dominicans Don't Play as rivals of Trinitarios.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips , Dominicans Don't Play , Tiny Rascal Gang

descriptions: Infobox lists Dominicans Don't Play as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Dominicans Don't Play

descriptions: Dominicans Don't Play is listed as a rival of Ñetas.
edges.evidence_quote: Estas pandillas rivales incluyen a United Blood Nation, Crips, 20 Luv, Dominican Power, Dominicans Don't Play, MS-13, Los Solidos, Grupo 27 y Latin Kings.

descriptions: PCC lists EPP as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Latin America : TCP , GDE , BDM , EPP , FARC , Tren de Aragua , Jalisco Cartel , Massa Carcerária [ pt ] , Primer Cartel Uruguayo.

descriptions: Burger Bar Boys listed as an ally of Tottenham Mandem in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Broadwater Farm Posse, Burger Bar Boys, Causeway Gang, Edmonton Firm (also known as The Firm, Edmonton Mandem, or EMD), Star Gang

descriptions: The Ciarelli clan ambushed the Di Rocco and Spinelli families in 1972.
edges.evidence_quote: quattro fratelli di una tribù nomade dei colli di Pescara (Ciarelli), armati di mitra, fucili e pistole, tendono un agguato a due tribù avversarie (Di Rocco e Spinelli)

descriptions: The Ciarelli clan ambushed the Di Rocco and Spinelli families in 1972.
edges.evidence_quote: quattro fratelli di una tribù nomade dei colli di Pescara (Ciarelli), armati di mitra, fucili e pistole, tendono un agguato a due tribù avversarie (Di Rocco e Spinelli)

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the SLA.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA)

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the SLA during the 1980s and 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA), eventually leading to Israel's withdrawal from southern Lebanon in 2000.

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the South Lebanon Army during the 1980s and 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA), eventually leading to Israel's withdrawal from southern Lebanon in 2000.

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the SLA, leading to its collapse.
edges.evidence_quote: With the collapse of the SLA, and the rapid advance of Hezbollah forces, Israel withdrew on 24 May 2000.

descriptions: Hezbollah fought against the SLA during the Israeli occupation.
edges.evidence_quote: Throughout the 1980s and 1990s, Hezbollah fought against Israeli forces and the South Lebanon Army (SLA), eventually leading to Israel's withdrawal from southern Lebanon in 2000.

edges.source: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine
descriptions: PFLP and SLA clashed in South Lebanon, resulting in PFLP casualties.
edges.evidence_quote: On 8 June 1990 a clash occurred near Shuwayya in South Lebanon in which four members of the PFLP were killed by the South Lebanon Army (SLA).

descriptions: Los Huistas imposed taxes on Tío Balde, causing tensions.
edges.evidence_quote: En 2024-2025, surgieron tensiones cuando Los Huistas comenzaron a cobrar 'impuestos' por el paso de drogas a otros grupos locales, como la banda de 'Tío Balde'.

descriptions: Tío Balde joined with CJNG to form Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala.
edges.evidence_quote: Esto llevó a que estos grupos locales se aliaran con el CJNG para formar el Cartel Chiapas-Guatemala

'Ndrina Costa.description: The Costa are a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Siderno, Italy. Initially allied with the Commisso clan, they later became rivals, leading to a violent feud in the 1980s and 1990s. The Costa were involved in drug trafficking with channels in Turin, Gorizia, and Modena. The conflict resulted in the deaths of several Costa members, and the clan was largely annihilated with help from Reggio Calabria clans like the Piromalli. Only two brothers, Giuseppe and Tommaso Costa, survived; Giuseppe later became a collaborator of justice.

descriptions: Costa 'ndrina is listed as a rival of Cordì 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cataldo 'ndrina , Mazzaferro 'ndrina , Costa 'ndrina, Ierinò 'ndrina

descriptions: The Curciarello were initially allies of the Costa, switched to Commisso during the feud, then returned to Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: I Curciarello sono stati inizialmente alleati dei Costa, con lo scatenarsi della faida negli anni 1980 sono passati ai Commisso per poi successivamente ritornare con i Costa.

descriptions: The Piromalli helped the Costa in their feud against the Commisso.
edges.evidence_quote: anche con l'aiuto di cosche reggine come i Piromalli , vengono uccisi anche gli ultimi esponenti che continuavano la lotta.

descriptions: The Sia are listed as allies of the Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Sia

descriptions: The Ursino are listed as allies of the Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Alleati: Ursino

descriptions: Costa and Commisso were in conflict in the Siderno feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Siderno : combattuta a partire dal 1987 quando la 'ndrina dei Costa volle rendersi autonoma dalla potente 'ndrina dei Commisso.

descriptions: The Costa later became a rival faction to the Commisso.
edges.evidence_quote: Successivamente diventano fazione rivale.

descriptions: The Costa were initially allies of the Commisso, later became rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: I Costa sono una 'ndrina di Siderno , inizialmente alleati dei Commisso . Successivamente diventano fazione rivale.

descriptions: Gli Ierinò sono alleati dei Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati dei Mazzaferro dei Cataldo e dei Costa .

descriptions: The Mazzaferro are allied with the Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Sono alleati con le 'ndrine degli Ierinò , dei Costa e dei Cataldo

descriptions: The Sia 'ndrina is allied with the Costa 'ndrina.
edges.evidence_quote: È alleata dei ... i Costa di Siderno

descriptions: The Costa 'ndrina challenged the Commisso 'ndrina for control of Siderno, leading to a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1980s, the Commisso were challenged by the Costa 'ndrina over the command of Siderno.

descriptions: Commisso was in a feud with Costa.
edges.evidence_quote: Alla fine degli anni ottanta scoppia la faida con la 'ndrina dei Costa

descriptions: AQIM listed Al-Mulathameen as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Al-Mulathameen (2012–2013)

descriptions: Al-Mulathameen was an ally of Boko Haram in 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Al-Mulathameen (2013)

descriptions: Riina distributed extortion payments to the Noce family.
edges.evidence_quote: e l’ultima parte alla Noce, ossia a Raffaele Ganci

Lopers 13.description: Lopers 13 is a Mexican American street gang founded in Santa Ana, California, in the late 1980s or early 1990s. It is the largest gang in Santa Ana by territory, controlling areas including Main Street, Minnie Street, First Street, and Edinger Avenue. The gang is involved in drug trafficking and has been targeted by law enforcement operations.

descriptions: Lopers 13 and Logan Street are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Walnut street, Logan street

descriptions: Lopers 13 and Walnut Street are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Walnut street, Logan street

Westmob.description: Westmob is a primarily African-American street gang based in San Francisco, California, active since at least 1999. It claims territory in the Hunters Point projects and is known for a long-running conflict with the rival Big Block gang. The gang has been linked to rap music and drug trafficking, and its rivalry was featured in the 2002 documentary Straight Outta Hunters Point.

descriptions: Westmob has been in conflict with Big Block since 1999.
edges.evidence_quote: Since 1999, they have been in conflict with the Big Block gang, which is its major rival in the area.

descriptions: Westmob associates with Oakdale Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Westmob is known to associate with the Oakdale Mob and Sunnydale Gangsters.

descriptions: Westmob associates with Sunnydale Gangsters.
edges.evidence_quote: Westmob is known to associate with the Oakdale Mob and Sunnydale Gangsters.

descriptions: Chief Squad is a splinter group of Baghdad Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Other splinter groups include ChinaSquadNyalenda Base, Chief Squad, Nyamasaria Massive, Kenda Kenda, Kondele Baghdad for Peace, Karamojong Boys, Saba Saba, Artur Margaryan, Kebago, and American Marines.

descriptions: Raymond Avenue Crips is listed as an ally of Tongan Crip Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sons of Samoa , Tongan Foe Lyfe Ganster Crip, Raymond Avenue Crips

descriptions: Tehrik-e-Khilafat Pakistan merged into ISIS–K in January 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: These groups were Tehrik-e-Khilafat Khorasan (TKK), Khilafat Afghan and Muslim Dost's group, Azizullah Haqqani's group, and Tehrik-e-Khilafat Pakistan (TKP)

descriptions: TKP merged to form ISIS–K.
edges.evidence_quote: ISIS–K, when it was officially declared in January 2015, was the product of the merger of Tehrik-e Khilafat Khorasan (TKK), Khilafat Afghan, Muslim Dost's group, Azizullah Haqqani's group, and Tehrik-e Khilafat Pakistan (TKP)

The Firm.description: English organized crime group based in Bethnal Green, London, led by the Kray twins from the late 1950s until their arrest in 1968. Involved in murder, armed robbery, arson, protection rackets, gambling, and assaults. The twins were convicted in 1969 and sentenced to life imprisonment.

descriptions: The Cotroni family used the Krays to sell stolen bonds in Britain.
edges.evidence_quote: The Montreal-based Cotroni crime family, the Canadian satellite of New York's Bonanno crime family, decided to sell the stolen bonds in Britain through the Krays.

descriptions: The two gangs were in a turf war, a conflict relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: The Richardson gang and the Kray twins were engaged in a turf war in the mid-to-late 1960s.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Kray Firm as rivals of the Richardson Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Kray Firm

descriptions: The New York Mafia gave Joey Pyle a nickname, indicating a connection between the Krays' associate and the Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: he was known as the "London Don of Dons" by the New York Mafia

descriptions: The Kray twins allied with the American Mafia for business ventures in London.
edges.evidence_quote: During the 1960s, the Kray twins formed an alliance with the Commission, the governing body of the American Mafia.

descriptions: The Krays and Foreman cooperated in a protection racket federation.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Richardsons were jailed in 1967, the Krays, the Nashes, Foreman and Pyle came to an informal agreement after a series of meetings about splitting the proceeds from their various protection rackets

descriptions: The Krays and Nashes cooperated in a protection racket federation.
edges.evidence_quote: After the Richardsons were jailed in 1967, the Krays, the Nashes, Foreman and Pyle came to an informal agreement after a series of meetings about splitting the proceeds from their various protection rackets

descriptions: The Richardson Gang were direct rivals to the Kray twins.
edges.evidence_quote: South London was controlled at that time by the Richardson Gang , who were seen as direct rivals to the Kray Twins.

descriptions: The Richardson Gang is described as a rival of the Krays' Firm.
edges.evidence_quote: At Shepton Mallet they met Charlie Richardson, another inmate, who later formed the Richardson Gang, a rival of the Krays’ Firm.

descriptions: Ronnie Kray killed a member of the Richardson Gang, indicating conflict between the two gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Ronnie shot and killed George Cornell, a member of the Richardson Gang, at the Blind Beggar pub in Whitechapel on 9 March 1966.

Los Ninis.description: Los Ninis are the main armed wing of the Los Chapitos faction of the Sinaloa Cartel, composed of young hitmen aged 20-35 trained in urban combat. They operate as security forces for Los Chapitos leaders, and are involved in torture, murder, drug trafficking, and controlling cartel territories. Based in Culiacán, Sinaloa, they were formed to maintain Los Chapitos' dominance over the Sinaloa Cartel.

descriptions: Los Ninis is rival to Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Beltrán-Leyva Cartel

descriptions: Los Ninis is allied with Gente Nueva.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Gente Nueva

descriptions: Los Ninis is rival to La Plaza Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... La Plaza Cartel

descriptions: Los Ninis is the armed wing of Los Chapitos faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Ninis are the main armed wing of the faction of the Sinaloa Cartel , known as Los Chapitos.

descriptions: Los Ninis is allied with Los Chimales.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Los Chimales

descriptions: Los Ninis prevailed over Los Dámasos in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: With him at the helm, they prevailed over " Los Dámasos "

descriptions: Los Ninis is rival to Los Guanos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Los Guanos

descriptions: Los Ninis is rival to Los Rusos.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Los Rusos

descriptions: Los Ninis is allied with the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Brindisi is listed as an allied 'ndrina of Santaiti.
edges.evidence_quote: 'Ndrine alleate - Brindisi

descriptions: Cardillo-Ranieri were scissionists who attacked Clan Mariano, leading to internal feud.
edges.evidence_quote: L'origine della faida, secondo la ricostruzione dei Magistrati, risalirebbe al 24 marzo del 1991, Domenica delle Palme , in un agguato operato da Luca Mariani e da suo cugino Simone Festa, entrambi affiliati agli scissionisti Cardillo-Ranieri, nel tentativo di uccidere Giovanni Sole (allora considerato il braccio destro di Ciro Mariano), ma riuscirono a colpire a morte solo il suo autista, Ciro Napoletano, mentre Vincenzo Romano, ferito, sopravvisse all'agguato.

descriptions: MOD was in dispute with Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: the M.O.D was involved in a dispute with two other gangs; the Laotian Posse and the Alliance.

descriptions: Bandidos and Westside Nation were in a conflict starting July 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2013, a conflict erupted between the Bandidos and the Westside Nation, a breakaway group of former members of the club's "Westside" chapter in Næstved who defected following a dispute over the club's national leadership.

descriptions: Some Westside Nation members joined Satudarah after disbanding.
edges.evidence_quote: The Westside Nation disbanded in 2014, with some members returning to the Bandidos and others patching over to Satudarah, forming the core of the club's Copenhagen chapter.

descriptions: D'Agostino are part of the group leading the Laureana di Borrello locale in Sardinia.
edges.evidence_quote: In Sardegna opera il locale di Laureana di Borrello , con a capo i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino.

descriptions: Bellocco supported the Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino group.
edges.evidence_quote: i Bellocco appoggiavano il secondo gruppo

descriptions: These two groups were opposed in the Faida di Laureana in the 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni ottanta quando nella faida di Laureana di Borrello aveva visto contrapposte le cosche Albanese-Cutellè-Tassone e i Ferrentino-Chindamo-Lamari-D'Agostino

'Ndrina Giacobbe.description: The 'Ndrina Giacobbe is a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Borgia, Calabria, Italy. It is allied with the Gaglianesi clan. After the death of boss Antonino Giacobbi, control passed to the Passafaro family, while the Pilò-Cossari remain criminal referents in the coastal area.

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Giacobbe is allied with the Cosca dei Gaglianesi.
edges.evidence_quote: La 'ndrina Giacobbi è una cosca malavitosa o 'ndrina della zona di Borgia alleata con la Cosca dei Gaglianesi in Calabria .

Kadyrovites.description: Informal Chechnya-based detachments of Russian state security forces, effectively serving as Ramzan Kadyrov's private army. Founded in the late 1990s, they have been accused of widespread human rights abuses including torture and extrajudicial killings. They have participated in conflicts such as the Syrian Civil War and the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

descriptions: Azov Regiment fighters prepared bullets to be used against Chechen troops, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The National Guard of Ukraine released a video appearing to show fighters from the Azov Regiment, based in Mariupol, greasing bullets in lard (salo) to be used against Chechen troops as an insult

descriptions: Chechen Death Battalion is a Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.

descriptions: Kadyrovites were formed from pardoned former militants of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2015 BBC reported that a considerable part of Kadyrovites were Chechen Republic of Ichkeria militants pardoned by Russian president Vladimir Putin under the word of Ramzan Kadyrov

descriptions: Kadyrovite troops executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic.
edges.evidence_quote: Ukrainian media reported that Chechen troops executed conscripted soldiers of the Luhansk People's Republic who refused to fight.

descriptions: Oil Regiment employees are Kadyrovites, indicating Oil Regiment is part of Kadyrovites.
edges.evidence_quote: According to some sources, Umarov's father, Khamad Umarov, was kidnapped back on 5 May 2008, by the Kadyrovite employees of the Oil Regiment (Neftepolk) headed by Chechnya's First Deputy Prime Minister Adam Delimkhanov.

descriptions: Special Battalion Zapad opposed Kadyrovites during a confrontation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kadyrovites ended up backing down in that confrontation when another Chechen warlord, Said-Magomed Kakiev, head of the Spetsnaz GRU unit the Special Battalion Zapad, came down on Baisarov's side.

descriptions: Vostok Battalion is a Chechen militant unit loyal to Kadyrov, part of Kadyrovite forces in Ukraine.
edges.evidence_quote: Chechen militants loyal to Kadyrov have been active in the Russo-Ukrainian War since 2014. These forces include the Vostok Battalion and the Chechen Death Battalion.

descriptions: Former Wagner fighters have joined Akhmat units.
edges.evidence_quote: Other reports indicate that former Wagner fighters have joined Chechen Akhmat units, whilst still wearing Wagner patches.
